engineGuide.h2: BMW X4 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW X4 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The N47D20 powered early F26 xDrive20d models, offering strong efficiency but gaining notoriety for its timing chain design flaw positioned at the rear of the engine.
compatibleVariants: xDrive20d, xDrive20d xLine, xDrive20d M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain wear / failure - chain positioned at flywheel side requires transmission removal for replacement; failure typically between 50,000-120,000 miles
EGR valve clogging and cooler leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The B47D20 replaced the N47 from 2014 onward, bringing revised timing chain architecture, improved thermal management, and Euro 6 emissions compliance. Early B47 units entered X4 production in late 2014 alongside N47-powered models.
compatibleVariants: xDrive20d (2014-2018), xDrive20d (G02, 2018-2024), xDrive20d M Sport, xDrive20d xLine, xDrive25d (G02)
commonFailures: EGR cooler deposits and airflow restriction - fault codes 290900 and 290B00 common; may require EGR cooler replacement and intake cleaning
DPF clogging in urban-use vehicles
MAF sensor contamination from crankcase vapours
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: A 3.0-litre straight-six turbodiesel offering exceptional torque and refinement. The N57 powered higher-spec F26 X4 models and is regarded as BMW's most reliable diesel family when properly maintained.
compatibleVariants: xDrive30d, xDrive30d M Sport, xDrive35d, xDrive35d M Sport
commonFailures: EGR valve and cooler failure - carbon buildup restricts flow
Timing chain stretch requiring replacement (labour-intensive due to flywheel-side positioning)
Intake manifold carbon deposits requiring cleaning every 50,000-60,000 miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for N57D30 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: BMW's next-generation modular diesel engine introduced with the G02 X4. Featuring sequential turbocharging (twin-scroll on M40d), improved thermal efficiency, and reduced internal friction compared to the N57.
compatibleVariants: xDrive30d, xDrive30d M Sport, M40d, M40d M Sport
commonFailures: Sequential turbo system wear (M40d) - complex multi-turbo configuration can develop actuator faults
EGR system deposits
AdBlue / SCR system failures affecting Euro 6 compliance
entries.cta: Get quotes for B57D30 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: A 2.0-litre twin-scroll turbocharged four-cylinder introducing BMW's TwinPower Turbo technology to the four-cylinder petrol range. Replaced by the B48 in later models.
compatibleVariants: xDrive20i, xDrive20i M Sport, xDrive28i, xDrive28i M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and guide failure - chain positioned at rear of engine
Valve cover gasket leaks (oil seepage from cover area) - typically from 30,000-40,000 miles
High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for N20B20 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: A 3.0-litre turbocharged straight-six that succeeded the N54. The N55 introduced a single twin-scroll turbocharger and Valvetronic variable valve lift, offering improved response and reliability.
compatibleVariants: xDrive35i, xDrive35i M Sport, M40i (pre-2018, N55 variant)
commonFailures: High oil consumption - piston ring design leading to carbon buildup and ring sticking
VANOS solenoid failure causing rough idle and power loss
Valve cover gasket oil leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for N55B30 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Part of BMW's modular B-series engine family (2015 onward). The B48 offers improved thermal management, a closed-deck block design, and reduced weight compared to the N20 it replaced. Fitted with TwinScroll turbocharger.
compatibleVariants: xDrive20i, xDrive20i M Sport, xDrive30i, xDrive30i M Sport
commonFailures: Coolant loss - expansion tank cap failure and small cooling system leaks
PCV valve integrated into valve cover - failure requires complete valve cover replacement
Oil filter housing gasket failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: BMW's flagship B-series inline-six petrol engine, introduced with the G02 X4 M40i. Featuring an advanced closed-deck block, direct and port injection, and a twin-scroll turbocharger. Highly regarded for tuning potential.
commonFailures: VANOS solenoid failure - O-ring deterioration causing power loss and rough idle (typically from 50,000-75,000 miles)
Oil consumption / PCV system failure - integrated PCV valve fails, requiring valve cover replacement
Coolant consumption - pressure cap or small system leaks requiring regular top-ups
entries.cta: Get quotes for B58B30 BMW X4 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L MHEV Petrol
entries.history: The B48B20 was adapted for the xDrive25e plug-in hybrid, combining the turbocharged 2.0-litre petrol engine with an electric motor. The system delivers combined output of 292hp with WLTP electric-only range capability.
commonFailures: High-voltage battery degradation affecting electric range
Hybrid system cooling pump failures
Combustion engine shares B48B20 failure profile (coolant loss, PCV, VANOS)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 (hybrid) BMW X4 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW X4 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW X4, particularly early F26 models with the N47 diesel engine, has a well-documented reputation for timing chain failures that can lead to catastrophic engine damage. Later B47 and B58 engines improved reliability but introduced their own issues around EGR coolers, coolant loss, and turbocharger wear on M Performance variants. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common X4 engine failures and help you decide whether repair or replacement delivers the best value for your situation.

problems.group: BMW X4 N47 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW X4 N47 Timing Chain Failure - Why Early Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: BMW X4 xDrive20d (2014-2018, F26 chassis, N47D20 engine)
problems.rootCause: The N47 engine's timing chain is positioned at the flywheel side (rear) of the engine. Chain stretch and guide deterioration cause slack, leading to rattling noises and eventual chain failure. When the chain snaps or jumps teeth, valves collide with pistons - requiring a full engine replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Timing Chain Replacement - Catch it early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New timing chain, tensioners, guides, and oil pump chain while engine still runs
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles if done before chain stretch causes sprocket wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt N47D20 engine with upgraded timing chain components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles expected; upgraded chain design addresses original flaw

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise from the rear of the engine on cold starts, act immediately. A preventative timing chain replacement at a specialist costs £1,200-£1,800 and saves the engine. Once the chain fails, replacement is the only option - a rebuilt engine with upgraded components from a specialist is dramatically cheaper than a dealer-fit new unit (£3,500-£5,500 vs £8,000-£12,000). On a 2014-2018 X4 xDrive20d worth £8,000-£12,000, investing in preventative chain work makes excellent financial sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47D20 engine prices for your BMW X4

problems.h4: BMW X4 EGR Cooler Failure - Diesel Emissions System Weakness
problems.rootCause: The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) cooler develops internal cracks or becomes clogged with carbon deposits. This can lead to coolant loss, white smoke from exhaust, reduced engine performance, and in severe cases, coolant entering the combustion chamber causing hydro-lock. EGR cooler leaks are a known weakness across BMW's modern diesel range .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, clean EGR valve, replace gaskets, reset adaptations

repairOptions.tier: EGR Cooler + Intake Manifold Cleaning - Severe carbon buildup
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler, walnut blast intake manifold and valves, replace all gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles with proper maintenance and regular long journeys

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After hydro-lock damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: New lease of life; upgraded cooler design available

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of coolant loss without visible external leaks, have the EGR cooler pressure tested. Replacement at a specialist (£550-£850) is the most cost-effective solution. If you've experienced repeated EGR failures, consider an EGR delete/remap (where legally compliant for off-road use) - but for road-legal X4s, stick to genuine or OEM-quality replacement coolers. If the failure caused coolant ingestion and bent connecting rods, a rebuilt engine is your only option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 EGR cooler replacement and engine options

problems.group: BMW X4 M40i / M40d Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: BMW X4 M40i / M40d Turbocharger Failure - Performance Variant Weakness
problems.rootCause: On M40d models, the sequential twin-turbo system's variable geometry turbine can develop actuator faults and bearing wear under sustained high load. On M40i, the single twin-scroll turbo experiences bearing wear and seal failure, often accelerated by extended oil change intervals or poor oil quality .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, rebuild or replace turbo unit, new gaskets and oil lines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After turbo failure debris
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt performance engine with upgraded turbo components
repairOptions.longevity: Full factory specification; upgraded components available

problems.recommendation: Performance X4s demand shorter oil change intervals - every 8,000-10,000 miles with BMW Longlife-04 spec oil. If you hear metallic rattling or whistling from the turbo, or notice blue exhaust smoke, have it inspected immediately. Turbo rebuild or replacement at a specialist (£1,400-£2,200) is significantly cheaper than dealer pricing. If the turbo has catastrophically failed and sent debris through the engine, a full replacement is required - budget for a rebuilt performance engine rather than a new dealer unit to save £5,000-£7,000.
problems.cta: Compare B58 and B57 performance engine prices for X4 M40i / M40d

problems.h4: BMW X4 Diesel DPF Blockage - Short Journey Consequences
problems.affectedModels: BMW X4 xDrive20d, xDrive25d, xDrive30d, M40d (all diesel variants, 2014-2024)
problems.rootCause: The Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) requires regular passive regeneration - sustained driving at 40+ mph for 20-30 minutes to burn off accumulated soot. X4s used primarily for short urban journeys never complete regeneration cycles, leading to progressive blockage, back-pressure buildup, and eventual DPF failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic tool initiates active regeneration; requires 60-minute motorway drive
repairOptions.longevity: Good for light blockage; needs repeating every 3,000-5,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, bake/clean in specialist machine, refit, reset adaptations
New DPF, pressure sensors, differential pipes, gaskets, software reset

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After engine damage from back-pressure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new DPF system, full emissions compliance
repairOptions.longevity: Complete restoration; new DPF and upgraded engine

problems.recommendation: If your X4 diesel shows a DPF warning light, take it for a 30-40 minute motorway run at 2,000-2,500 RPM before seeking professional help. If the light persists, a forced regeneration at a specialist (£100-£180) is cheapest. For persistent blockages, professional DPF cleaning (£300-£500) is more cost-effective than replacement. Only replace the DPF if it's physically cracked or ash-load is beyond recovery (typically 120,000+ miles). On a high-mileage X4, consider whether the car's value justifies a DPF replacement - a rebuilt engine may be better value if other issues are present.
problems.cta: Get DPF cleaning and engine replacement quotes for your X4 diesel

problems.group: BMW X4 B58 Coolant Loss & PCV System Failure
problems.h4: BMW X4 B58 Coolant Loss & PCV System Failure - Petrol Performance Issues
problems.rootCause: The B58 engine has two well-documented issues. First, the coolant expansion tank cap can fail, allowing pressure loss and gradual coolant consumption without visible external leaks. Second, the PCV valve is integrated into the plastic valve cover - when the diaphragm fails, it causes whistling noises, oil consumption, and can pressurise the crankcase .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace expansion tank cap with updated design
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if no other leaks present

repairOptions.tier: Valve Cover & PCV Assembly Replacement - Moderate
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete valve cover assembly (PCV integrated), new gaskets

repairOptions.tier: Cooling System Pressure Test & Repair - Active leaks
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pressure test system, replace leaking hoses, water pump, or thermostat

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After head gasket failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B58 engine with upgraded PCV and cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; upgraded components address original weaknesses

problems.recommendation: B58 engines are generally robust, but coolant consumption should never be ignored. Replace the expansion tank cap as a first step (£25-£40). If you hear a whistling sound from the engine bay, the PCV diaphragm has likely failed - on B58 engines, this means replacing the entire valve cover assembly. Use a specialist (£550-£850) rather than a dealer (£900-£1,400). If the engine has overheated due to coolant loss, check for head gasket failure before committing to repair - on a high-mileage M40i, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components may be better value than repairing a compromised unit.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B58 engine prices for BMW X4 M40i

problems.group: BMW X4 High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure
problems.h4: BMW X4 High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure - Diesel & Petrol Risk
problems.affectedModels: BMW X4 xDrive20d, xDrive30d (N47, B47, N57, B57), xDrive20i, xDrive30i (B48), M40i (B58)
problems.rootCause: The HPFP's internal components wear over time, particularly with poor fuel quality or contaminated fuel. When the HPFP fails, it sends metallic debris through the entire fuel system - injectors, fuel rails, and return lines - requiring a complete fuel system replacement alongside the pump itself .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, flush fuel system, replace fuel filter
repairOptions.longevity: Good if debris hasn't reached injectors

repairOptions.tier: Complete Fuel System Replacement - After catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: HPFP, all injectors, fuel rail, high-pressure lines, tank clean

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After debris ingestion
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new complete fuel system

problems.recommendation: Use premium fuel from branded stations and replace the fuel filter every 40,000-60,000 miles. If you experience long cranking times, loss of power, or misfires, have fuel pressure tested immediately. Early HPFP replacement at a specialist (£700-£1,100) saves the rest of the fuel system. Once metal debris has reached the injectors, a complete fuel system replacement is unavoidable - and on a higher-mileage X4, a rebuilt engine with a complete new fuel system may be surprisingly cost-competitive (£4,000-£7,000).
problems.cta: Get quotes for HPFP replacement and rebuilt engine options

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW X4
finalCta.paragraph: From N47 timing chain failures to B58 coolant loss and M40d turbo issues, BMW X4 engines have specific weak points that can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK BMW X4 specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to address these original failure points - all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices across our nationwide network and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW X4 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW X4 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW X4 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW X4 across both generations (F26 2014-2018, G02 2018-2024) is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: BMW X4 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel powered the majority of UK X4 sales across both generations, dominating replacement demand. Early F26 models used the N47D20 (184hp), notorious for rear-mounted timing chain failures . From late 2014 onward, the improved B47D20 (188-190hp) introduced revised timing chain architecture and Euro 6 compliance, while G02 models added mild hybrid technology (MHT) from 2021, featuring a 48V starter-generator providing an 11hp boost during acceleration .
commonFailurePoints: N47 timing chain stretch (50k-120k miles); B47 EGR cooler deposits
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X4 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X4 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six turbodiesel delivered refined performance for xDrive30d and M40d variants across both X4 generations. F26 models used the N57D30 (258hp), praised for reliability but prone to EGR cooler carbon buildup. The G02 generation introduced the B57D30 family, with the xDrive30d producing 265hp and the M40d featuring sequential twin-turbocharging for 340hp .
commonFailurePoints: cooler carbon deposits; N57 timing chain stretch (labour-intensive due to flywheel positioning); B57 sequential turbo actuator faults
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X4 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X4 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine served as the entry point for X4 petrol variants across both generations. F26 models used the N20B20 (181-241hp), known for rear-mounted timing chain stretch and valve cover gasket oil leaks. G02 models replaced it with the improved B48B20 (184-252hp), featuring better thermal management, reduced internal friction, and a twin-scroll turbocharger .
commonFailurePoints: N20 timing chain stretch; valve cover gasket oil leaks (N20); B48 coolant loss from expansion tank cap failure; PCV valve failure requiring complete valve cover replacement
items.cta: Compare BMW X4 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X4 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six turbo petrol powered the M40i flagship across both X4 generations. F26 models (pre-2018) used the N55B30 (306-355hp), which succeeded the N54 with a single twin-scroll turbo and Valvetronic variable valve lift, but suffered from oil consumption due to piston ring carbon buildup. The G02 generation introduced the B58B30 (355-382hp), featuring a closed-deck block design, improved thermal management, and direct/port injection .
commonFailurePoints: N55 oil consumption (piston ring carbon buildup); VANOS solenoid failure (both N55
B58); B58 coolant loss from pressure cap
small cooling system leaks; integrated PCV failure on B58 requiring valve cover replacement
items.cta: Compare BMW X4 3.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW X4 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW X4 2.0L MHEV Petrol Engines
items.description: The mild hybrid electric vehicle (MHEV) system was introduced on G02 LCI models from 2021, pairing the B48B20 2.0-litre turbo petrol engine with a 48V starter-generator. The system recovers energy during braking to support electrical loads and provides an 11hp (8kW) boost during acceleration, improving throttle response and reducing turbo lag. The xDrive25e plug-in hybrid variant combines the B48 engine with an electric motor for a combined 292hp system output .
commonFailurePoints: High-voltage battery degradation affecting electric range (PHEV); hybrid system cooling pump failures; B48 base engine issues (coolant loss
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X4 2.0L MHEV engine availability

engineYears.h2: BMW X4 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the BMW X4's production run (2014-2025), helping owners identify their exact unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first-generation X4 (F26) launched in July 2014 with a diesel-only UK lineup - the N47-powered xDrive20d arrived carrying the rear-mounted timing chain design that would dominate replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Model launched July 2014 with coupé-SUV styling based on X3 (F25)
UK market initially received diesel engines only
xDrive20d offered with 6-speed manual or 8-speed automatic
xDrive30d and xDrive35d came exclusively with 8-speed ZF automatic
xLine and M Sport trim levels introduced alongside base SE
popularModels: BMW X4 xDrive20d SE · BMW X4 xDrive20d xLine · BMW X4 xDrive20d M Sport
BMW X4 xDrive30d M Sport · BMW X4 xDrive35d M Sport
knownFor: N47 timing chain failure risk (rear-mounted chain, 50k-120k miles) - X4's most searched replacement driver
B47 introduced late 2014 with revised timing chain architecture and Euro 6 compliance
Excellent motorway refinement and 45-55mpg real-world economy from 2.0L diesels
F26 platform shared with X3 (F25) but unique rear suspension tuning for sportier handling

N55B30 arrived in xDrive35i (306hp) and pre-2018 M40i (355hp)

B47D20 fully replaced N47D20 on xDrive20d production

BMW X4 xDrive30d · BMW X4 xDrive35i · BMW X4 M40i

N20 petrol engines share rear timing chain weakness - replacement demand growing
N55B30 M40i offers 355hp and 0-60 in 4.7 seconds but oil consumption issues reported
xDrive35d (313hp, 630Nm torque) remains rare but highly sought by towing users

faq.h2: BMW X4 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW X4 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt BMW X4 engine typically costs £1,900-£8,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Specialist fitting adds £450-£800.
keyPoints: N47/B47 2.0L diesel rebuild: £1,900-£4,200 supply-only
B58 3.0L petrol (M40i): £4,500-£8,500 supply-only
Specialist fitting nationwide: £450-£800 added to engine price
Main dealer replacement can cost 2-3x more than specialist rebuilt units
items.cta: Get a fixed-price quote for your BMW X4 engine replacement today

items.question: Is the BMW X4 N47 diesel engine reliable? What are the timing chain risks?
items.answer: The N47 engine has a well-documented timing chain weakness. Chain is rear-mounted and can fail between 50k-120k miles, potentially destroying the engine.
keyPoints: Metallic rattling on cold start is the primary warning sign
Chain stretch can cause the engine to jump timing without warning
Preventative replacement costs £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist
Post-failure engine replacement costs £3,500-£5,500 for a rebuilt unit
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N47 engine prices for your X4

items.question: What are the common problems with BMW X4 B47 diesel engines?
items.answer: The B47 is significantly more reliable than the N47, with the timing chain moved to the front. However, EGR cooler failures and DPF clogging are the main issues on urban-driven cars.
keyPoints: EGR valve and cooler failures can trigger limp mode and warning lights
DPF clogging affects cars used mainly for short journeys without regeneration cycles
Turbocharger wear is possible if oil changes are neglected
B47 regularly exceeds 120,000 miles without major issues when properly maintained
items.cta: Get B47 engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Which BMW X4 petrol engine is most reliable - B48 or N20?
items.answer: The B48 petrol engine is significantly more reliable than the N20. The N20 has a known rear-mounted timing chain weakness, while the B48 is part of BMW's improved modular engine family.
keyPoints: N20 timing chain stretch affects many pre-2018 X4 xDrive20i and xDrive28i
B48 features improved thermal management and closed-deck block design
B48 common issues: coolant cap failure and integrated PCV valve failure
B48 regularly reaches 150,000+ miles with proper servicing
items.cta: Compare B48 and N20 engine replacement costs for your X4

items.question: Is the BMW X4 M40i B58 engine reliable for high mileage?
items.answer: The B58 is widely considered BMW's most reliable modern six-cylinder petrol engine. With proper maintenance, it routinely exceeds 150,000-200,000 miles without major failures.
keyPoints: Closed-deck block design provides exceptional structural strength
Common minor issues: coolant cap leaks and VANOS solenoid wear
PCV valve integrated into valve cover requires full cover replacement when it fails
Well-maintained B58 engines last 300,000+ km before major work is needed
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt B58 engine for your X4 M40i

items.question: Can I fit a used BMW X4 engine instead of a rebuilt one?
items.answer: Yes, used engines cost £1,200-£3,500 supply-only. However, they offer no warranty on internal condition and may carry the same original failure risks.
keyPoints: Used engines are 40-60% cheaper than rebuilt units upfront
No guarantee on timing chain wear, injector health, or turbo condition
Rebuilt engines address original failure points with upgraded components
All EngineMarket rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt engine prices for your BMW X4

items.question: How long does a BMW X4 engine replacement take with supply and fit?
items.answer: A specialist can typically complete a BMW X4 engine replacement in 3-5 working days, including delivery of the rebuilt engine and professional fitting.
keyPoints: Engine delivery to your chosen garage takes 1-2 working days
Fitting time varies by variant: 2-3 days for 4-cylinder, 3-4 days for 6-cylinder
ISN coding and immobiliser matching adds 1 day for ECU synchronisation
Most specialists offer courtesy cars or collection/delivery services
items.cta: Book supply and fit for your BMW X4 engine replacement

items.question: Does BMW X4 engine replacement include ECU coding and ISN matching?
items.answer: Professional replacement includes ISN (Individual Serial Number) coding to match the replacement engine to your X4's immobiliser and ECU. Never skip this step.
keyPoints: ISN coding ensures the engine starts and runs with your car's security system
Without correct coding, the immobiliser will prevent engine operation
All EngineMarket specialists include ISN matching and DDE calibration
AdBlue/SCR system recalibration is also performed for diesel X4s
items.cta: Find a specialist who includes full ECU coding with your X4 engine

items.question: What causes a BMW X4 diesel to lose power with a check engine light?
items.answer: The most common cause is a faulty exhaust back-pressure sensor or throttle valve, which prevents proper DPF regeneration and triggers reduced power mode.
keyPoints: Faulty back-pressure sensor causes DPF regeneration to fail, leading to blockages
Throttle valve failure on 2020+ models triggers reduced power and check engine light
Sensor replacement typically costs £200-£300 including parts and labour
Ignoring the issue can lead to full DPF replacement costing £1,100-£1,800
items.cta: Get diagnostic and repair quotes for your BMW X4

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage BMW X4?
items.answer: Yes, if the body and transmission are sound. A £2,500-£5,500 rebuilt engine can add 100,000+ miles to a well-maintained X4 - far cheaper than replacing the whole vehicle.
keyPoints: A 2014-2018 X4 with 120k miles is worth £8,000-£12,000 - replacement makes financial sense
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing original failure points
Compare to buying another used X4 with unknown engine history
Typical rebuilt engine lifespan: 100,000+ miles with proper maintenance
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine prices for your BMW X4 model year
